# Generated by openai (gpt-3.5-turbo)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export function verifyAuth(req: NextRequest) {
  const { cookies } = req;

  if (!cookies.authToken) {
    return NextResponse.redirect('/login');
  }

  // Check if auth token is valid (e.g., verify token with authentication server)

  return true; // Or false if authentication fails
}

export function handleAuth(req: NextRequest) {
  if (verifyAuth(req)) {
    // User is authenticated, allow the request to continue
    return NextResponse.next();
  } else {
    // User is not authenticated, deny access
    return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 });
  }
}

async function authenticationMiddleware(req: NextRequest) {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }

  return handleAuth(req);
}

export default authenticationMiddleware;
